corrections.text: The article's 'What We Know' section states 'According to The Next Web, court records indicate that Meta employees torrented roughly 82 terabytes of pirated material.' In The Next Web source, the 82 terabytes figure (citing Tom's Hardware) refers to piracy volume established in the prior Kadrey v. Meta case filed in 2023, not to the new Elsevier et al. lawsuit filed on May 5, 2026. The new publisher complaint, per Variety, alleges Meta and Zuckerberg authorized the torrenting of over 267 terabytes of pirated material.
